name: vscode-systemrdl-pro
description: Live diagnostics + interactive memory-map viewer for SystemRDL 2.0

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with SystemRDL Pro
walkthroughs.description: Install the LSP backend, open a sample register map, and try the viewer.

steps.title: Install the LSP backend
steps.description: SystemRDL Pro calls Python on activation. Install the LSP module: `pip install systemrdl-lsp`. The extension shows an actionable banner if it's missing.

steps.title: Open the Memory Map
steps.description: Open any `.rdl` file and press **Ctrl+Shift+V** (Cmd+Shift+V on macOS), or run **SystemRDL: Show Memory Map** from the command palette.

steps.title: Filter & navigate
steps.description: **Cmd-F** in the viewer focuses the filter input — narrow by name, address (`0x10`), or field. Click any register to scroll the editor; cursor in the editor auto-selects the matching tree node.

steps.title: Right-click for clipboard actions
steps.description: Right-click any tree row for **Copy Name / Copy Address / Copy Type / Reveal in Editor**. Useful when generating documentation or test stimuli.

systemrdl-pro.pythonPath.description: Path to Python interpreter that has systemrdl-lsp installed. Empty = use fallback chain (ms-python.python → PATH).

systemrdl-pro.includePaths.description: Directories searched by `include directives. Workspace-relative paths supported.

systemrdl-pro.includeVars.description: Variables expanded inside `include "..." paths. Use $NAME or ${NAME} in the include directive; values come from this map first, then process environment as fallback. Example: { "IP_ROOT": "/home/user/lib" } lets you write `include "$IP_ROOT/common.rdl".

systemrdl-pro.perlSafeOpcodes.description: Override the Perl `Safe` opcode set passed to the SystemRDL Perl preprocessor. Empty = compiler default (covers ~95% of macros). Add `:base_io` to allow `print`-based code generation. Example: [":base_core", ":base_mem", ":base_loop", ":base_orig", ":base_math", ":base_io"].

systemrdl-pro.preindex.enabled.description: Pre-elaborate every `.rdl` file in the workspace on startup so workspace-wide search (Ctrl+T) finds symbols without first opening the source. Off by default — multi-window setups can peg the CPU. Enable if you rely on workspace-wide symbol search.

systemrdl-pro.preindex.maxFiles.description: Cap on the number of `.rdl` files the pre-index walks. Beyond this, additional files are loaded lazily on first edit. Tune up for big monorepos at the cost of higher startup memory.

systemrdl-pro.viewer.colors.description: Override viewer access-mode colours and chrome tokens. Each key maps to a CSS custom property (`--rdl-acc-rw`, `--rdl-acc-ro`, …). Values are any valid CSS colour. Empty = use the built-in palette. Recognised keys: `rw`, `ro`, `wo`, `w1c`, `rsv`, `accent`, `warning`, `bg`, `panel`, `chrome`, `border`, `fg`, `dim`, `selected`.

systemrdl-pro.trace.server.description: Trace LSP communication (output to 'SystemRDL Pro' channel).
